speaker: DUKE
text: So then you hope of pardon from Lord Angelo?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: The miserable have no other medicine

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: But only hope.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: I have hope to live and am prepared to die.

speaker: DUKE
text: Be absolute for death. Either death or life

speaker: DUKE
text: Shall thereby be the sweeter. Reason thus with life:

speaker: DUKE
text: If I do lose thee, I do lose a thing

speaker: DUKE
text: That none but fools would keep. A breath thou art,

speaker: DUKE
text: Servile to all the skyey influences

speaker: DUKE
text: That doth this habitation where thou keep’st

speaker: DUKE
text: Hourly afflict. Merely, thou art death’s fool,

speaker: DUKE
text: For him thou labor’st by thy flight to shun,

speaker: DUKE
text: And yet runn’st toward him still. Thou art not noble,

speaker: DUKE
text: For all th’ accommodations that thou bear’st

speaker: DUKE
text: Are nursed by baseness. Thou ’rt by no means valiant,

speaker: DUKE
text: For thou dost fear the soft and tender fork

speaker: DUKE
text: Of a poor worm. Thy best of rest is sleep,

speaker: DUKE
text: And that thou oft provok’st, yet grossly fear’st

speaker: DUKE
text: Thy death, which is no more. Thou art not thyself,

speaker: DUKE
text: For thou exists on many a thousand grains

speaker: DUKE
text: That issue out of dust. Happy thou art not,

speaker: DUKE
text: For what thou hast not, still thou striv’st to get,

speaker: DUKE
text: And what thou hast, forget’st. Thou art not certain,

speaker: DUKE
text: For thy complexion shifts to strange effects

speaker: DUKE
text: After the moon. If thou art rich, thou ’rt poor,

speaker: DUKE
text: For, like an ass whose back with ingots bows,

speaker: DUKE
text: Thou bear’st thy heavy riches but a journey,

speaker: DUKE
text: And death unloads thee. Friend hast thou none,

speaker: DUKE
text: For thine own bowels which do call thee sire,

speaker: DUKE
text: The mere effusion of thy proper loins,

speaker: DUKE
text: Do curse the gout, serpigo, and the rheum

speaker: DUKE
text: For ending thee no sooner. Thou hast nor youth nor age,

speaker: DUKE
text: But as it were an after-dinner’s sleep

speaker: DUKE
text: Dreaming on both, for all thy blessèd youth

speaker: DUKE
text: Becomes as agèd and doth beg the alms

speaker: DUKE
text: Of palsied eld; and when thou art old and rich,

speaker: DUKE
text: Thou hast neither heat, affection, limb, nor beauty

speaker: DUKE
text: To make thy riches pleasant. What’s yet in this

speaker: DUKE
text: That bears the name of life? Yet in this life

speaker: DUKE
text: Lie hid more thousand deaths; yet death we fear,

speaker: DUKE
text: That makes these odds all even.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: I humbly thank you.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: To sue to live, I find I seek to die,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: And seeking death, find life. Let it come on.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: What ho! Peace here, grace, and good company.

speaker: PROVOST
text: Who’s there? Come in. The wish deserves a welcome.

speaker: DUKE
text: Dear sir, ere long I’ll visit you again.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Most holy sir, I thank you.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: My business is a word or two with Claudio.

speaker: PROVOST
text: And very welcome.—Look, signior, here’s your sister.

speaker: DUKE
text: Provost, a word with you.

speaker: PROVOST
text: As many as you please.

speaker: DUKE
text: Bring me to hear them speak, where I may be concealed.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Now, sister, what’s the comfort?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Why,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: As all comforts are, most good, most good indeed.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Lord Angelo, having affairs to heaven,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Intends you for his swift ambassador,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Where you shall be an everlasting leiger;

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Therefore your best appointment make with speed.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Tomorrow you set on.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Is there no remedy?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: None but such remedy as, to save a head,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To cleave a heart in twain.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: But is there any?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Yes, brother, you may live.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: There is a devilish mercy in the judge,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: If you’ll implore it, that will free your life

speaker: ISABELLA
text: But fetter you till death.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Perpetual durance?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Ay, just; perpetual durance, a restraint,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Though all the world’s vastidity you had,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To a determined scope.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: But in what nature?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: In such a one as, you consenting to ’t,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Would bark your honor from that trunk you bear

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And leave you naked.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Let me know the point.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: O, I do fear thee, Claudio, and I quake

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Lest thou a feverous life shouldst entertain,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And six or seven winters more respect

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Than a perpetual honor. Dar’st thou die?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: The sense of death is most in apprehension,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And the poor beetle that we tread upon

speaker: ISABELLA
text: In corporal sufferance finds a pang as great

speaker: ISABELLA
text: As when a giant dies.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Why give you me this shame?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Think you I can a resolution fetch

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: From flowery tenderness? If I must die,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: I will encounter darkness as a bride,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: And hug it in mine arms.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: There spake my brother! There my father’s grave

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Did utter forth a voice. Yes, thou must die.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Thou art too noble to conserve a life

speaker: ISABELLA
text: In base appliances. This outward-sainted deputy—

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Whose settled visage and deliberate word

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Nips youth i’ th’ head, and follies doth enew

speaker: ISABELLA
text: As falcon doth the fowl—is yet a devil.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: His filth within being cast, he would appear

speaker: ISABELLA
text: A pond as deep as hell.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: The prenzie Angelo?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: O, ’tis the cunning livery of hell

speaker: ISABELLA
text: The damned’st body to invest and cover

speaker: ISABELLA
text: In prenzie guards. Dost thou think, Claudio,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: If I would yield him my virginity

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Thou mightst be freed?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: O heavens, it cannot be!

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Yes, he would give ’t thee; from this rank offense,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: So to offend him still. This night’s the time

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That I should do what I abhor to name,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Or else thou diest tomorrow.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Thou shalt not do ’t.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: O, were it but my life,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I’d throw it down for your deliverance

speaker: ISABELLA
text: As frankly as a pin.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Thanks, dear Isabel.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Be ready, Claudio, for your death tomorrow.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Yes. Has he affections in him

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: That thus can make him bite the law by th’ nose,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: When he would force it? Sure it is no sin,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Or of the deadly seven it is the least.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Which is the least?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: If it were damnable, he being so wise,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Why would he for the momentary trick

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Be perdurably fined? O, Isabel—

speaker: ISABELLA
text: What says my brother?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Death is a fearful thing.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And shamèd life a hateful.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Ay, but to die, and go we know not where,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: To lie in cold obstruction and to rot,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: This sensible warm motion to become

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: A kneaded clod; and the delighted spirit

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: To bathe in fiery floods, or to reside

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: In thrilling region of thick-ribbèd ice,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: To be imprisoned in the viewless winds

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: And blown with restless violence round about

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: The pendent world; or to be worse than worst

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Of those that lawless and incertain thought

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Imagine howling—’tis too horrible.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: The weariest and most loathèd worldly life

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: That age, ache, penury, and imprisonment

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Can lay on nature is a paradise

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: To what we fear of death.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Alas, alas!

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Sweet sister, let me live.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: What sin you do to save a brother’s life,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Nature dispenses with the deed so far

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: That it becomes a virtue.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: O, you beast!

speaker: ISABELLA
text: O faithless coward, O dishonest wretch,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Wilt thou be made a man out of my vice?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Is ’t not a kind of incest to take life

speaker: ISABELLA
text: From thine own sister’s shame? What should I think?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Heaven shield my mother played my father fair,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: For such a warpèd slip of wilderness

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Ne’er issued from his blood. Take my defiance;

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Die, perish. Might but my bending down

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Reprieve thee from thy fate, it should proceed.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I’ll pray a thousand prayers for thy death,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: No word to save thee.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Nay, hear me, Isabel—

speaker: ISABELLA
text: O, fie, fie, fie!

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Thy sin’s not accidental, but a trade.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Mercy to thee would prove itself a bawd.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: ’Tis best that thou diest quickly.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: O, hear me, Isabella—

speaker: DUKE
text: Vouchsafe a word, young sister, but one word.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: What is your will?

speaker: DUKE
text: Might you dispense with your leisure, I

speaker: DUKE
text: would by and by have some speech with you. The

speaker: DUKE
text: satisfaction I would require is likewise your own

speaker: DUKE
text: benefit.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I have no superfluous leisure. My stay must

speaker: ISABELLA
text: be stolen out of other affairs, but I will attend you

speaker: ISABELLA
text: awhile.

speaker: DUKE
text: Son, I have overheard

speaker: DUKE
text: what hath passed between you and your

speaker: DUKE
text: sister. Angelo had never the purpose to corrupt her;

speaker: DUKE
text: only he hath made an assay of her virtue, to practice

speaker: DUKE
text: his judgment with the disposition of natures. She,

speaker: DUKE
text: having the truth of honor in her, hath made him

speaker: DUKE
text: that gracious denial which he is most glad to

speaker: DUKE
text: receive. I am confessor to Angelo, and I know this

speaker: DUKE
text: to be true. Therefore prepare yourself to death. Do

speaker: DUKE
text: not satisfy your resolution with hopes that are

speaker: DUKE
text: fallible. Tomorrow you must die. Go to your knees

speaker: DUKE
text: and make ready.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Let me ask my sister pardon. I am so out of

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: love with life that I will sue to be rid of it.

speaker: DUKE
text: Hold you there. Farewell.—Provost, a

speaker: DUKE
text: word with you.

speaker: PROVOST
text: What’s your will, father?

speaker: DUKE
text: That now you are come, you will be

speaker: DUKE
text: gone. Leave me awhile with the maid. My mind

speaker: DUKE
text: promises with my habit no loss shall touch her by

speaker: DUKE
text: my company.

speaker: PROVOST
text: In good time.

speaker: DUKE
text: The hand that hath made

speaker: DUKE
text: you fair hath made you good. The goodness that is

speaker: DUKE
text: cheap in beauty makes beauty brief in goodness,

speaker: DUKE
text: but grace, being the soul of your complexion, shall

speaker: DUKE
text: keep the body of it ever fair. The assault that Angelo

speaker: DUKE
text: hath made to you, fortune hath conveyed to my

speaker: DUKE
text: understanding; and but that frailty hath examples

speaker: DUKE
text: for his falling, I should wonder at Angelo. How will

speaker: DUKE
text: you do to content this substitute and to save your

speaker: DUKE
text: brother?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I am now going to resolve him. I had rather

speaker: ISABELLA
text: my brother die by the law than my son should be

speaker: ISABELLA
text: unlawfully born. But, O, how much is the good

speaker: ISABELLA
text: duke deceived in Angelo! If ever he return, and I

speaker: ISABELLA
text: can speak to him, I will open my lips in vain, or

speaker: ISABELLA
text: discover his government.

speaker: DUKE
text: That shall not be much amiss. Yet, as

speaker: DUKE
text: the matter now stands, he will avoid your accusation:

speaker: DUKE
text: he made trial of you only. Therefore, fasten

speaker: DUKE
text: your ear on my advisings. To the love I have in doing

speaker: DUKE
text: good, a remedy presents itself. I do make myself

speaker: DUKE
text: believe that you may most uprighteously do a poor

speaker: DUKE
text: wronged lady a merited benefit, redeem your brother

speaker: DUKE
text: from the angry law, do no stain to your own

speaker: DUKE
text: gracious person, and much please the absent duke,

speaker: DUKE
text: if peradventure he shall ever return to have hearing

speaker: DUKE
text: of this business.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Let me hear you speak farther. I have spirit to

speaker: ISABELLA
text: do anything that appears not foul in the truth of my

speaker: ISABELLA
text: spirit.

speaker: DUKE
text: Virtue is bold, and goodness never

speaker: DUKE
text: fearful. Have you not heard speak of Mariana, the

speaker: DUKE
text: sister of Frederick, the great soldier who miscarried

speaker: DUKE
text: at sea?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I have heard of the lady, and good words

speaker: ISABELLA
text: went with her name.

speaker: DUKE
text: She should this Angelo have married,

speaker: DUKE
text: was affianced to her oath, and the nuptial appointed.

speaker: DUKE
text: Between which time of the contract and

speaker: DUKE
text: limit of the solemnity, her brother Frederick was

speaker: DUKE
text: wracked at sea, having in that perished vessel the

speaker: DUKE
text: dowry of his sister. But mark how heavily this befell

speaker: DUKE
text: to the poor gentlewoman. There she lost a noble

speaker: DUKE
text: and renowned brother, in his love toward her ever

speaker: DUKE
text: most kind and natural; with him, the portion and

speaker: DUKE
text: sinew of her fortune, her marriage dowry; with

speaker: DUKE
text: both, her combinate husband, this well-seeming

speaker: DUKE
text: Angelo.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Can this be so? Did Angelo so leave her?

speaker: DUKE
text: Left her in her tears and dried not one

speaker: DUKE
text: of them with his comfort, swallowed his vows

speaker: DUKE
text: whole, pretending in her discoveries of dishonor; in

speaker: DUKE
text: few, bestowed her on her own lamentation, which

speaker: DUKE
text: she yet wears for his sake; and he, a marble to her

speaker: DUKE
text: tears, is washed with them but relents not.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: What a merit were it in death to take this

speaker: ISABELLA
text: poor maid from the world! What corruption in this

speaker: ISABELLA
text: life, that it will let this man live! But how out of this

speaker: ISABELLA
text: can she avail?

speaker: DUKE
text: It is a rupture that you may easily heal,

speaker: DUKE
text: and the cure of it not only saves your brother, but

speaker: DUKE
text: keeps you from dishonor in doing it.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Show me how, good father.

speaker: DUKE
text: This forenamed maid hath yet in her

speaker: DUKE
text: the continuance of her first affection. His unjust

speaker: DUKE
text: unkindness, that in all reason should have

speaker: DUKE
text: quenched her love, hath, like an impediment in the

speaker: DUKE
text: current, made it more violent and unruly. Go you to

speaker: DUKE
text: Angelo, answer his requiring with a plausible obedience,

speaker: DUKE
text: agree with his demands to the point. Only

speaker: DUKE
text: refer yourself to this advantage: first, that your stay

speaker: DUKE
text: with him may not be long, that the time may have all

speaker: DUKE
text: shadow and silence in it, and the place answer to

speaker: DUKE
text: convenience. This being granted in course, and

speaker: DUKE
text: now follows all: we shall advise this wronged maid

speaker: DUKE
text: to stead up your appointment, go in your place. If

speaker: DUKE
text: the encounter acknowledge itself hereafter, it may

speaker: DUKE
text: compel him to her recompense; and here, by this, is

speaker: DUKE
text: your brother saved, your honor untainted, the poor

speaker: DUKE
text: Mariana advantaged, and the corrupt deputy

speaker: DUKE
text: scaled. The maid will I frame and make fit for his

speaker: DUKE
text: attempt. If you think well to carry this as you may,

speaker: DUKE
text: the doubleness of the benefit defends the deceit

speaker: DUKE
text: from reproof. What think you of it?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: The image of it gives me content already, and

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I trust it will grow to a most prosperous perfection.

speaker: DUKE
text: It lies much in your holding up. Haste

speaker: DUKE
text: you speedily to Angelo. If for this night he entreat

speaker: DUKE
text: you to his bed, give him promise of satisfaction. I

speaker: DUKE
text: will presently to Saint Luke’s. There at the moated

speaker: DUKE
text: grange resides this dejected Mariana. At that place

speaker: DUKE
text: call upon me, and dispatch with Angelo that it may

speaker: DUKE
text: be quickly.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I thank you for this comfort. Fare you well,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: good father.
